event: STM32 flight controller lead times extend to 18-22 weeks by Q3 2026 as Drone Dominance (200K units) and Ukraine FPV production (10K+/month) compound demand on 267 STM32-based FC designs (81% of 328 tracked platforms)
evidence_basis: Forge DB shows 267/328 flight controllers use STM32 MCUs (81% concentration). Existing flags document Drone Dominance targeting 200K drones and Ukraine producing 10K+ FPV drones monthly. Both programs use STM32-based FCs per 'Ukraine and US Drone Dominance compete for same FPV components' flag. No current lead time tracking exists for this critical bottleneck.
probability_basis: High confidence in demand (verified programs), medium confidence in lead time estimate (inferred from compound demand without supplier capacity data)
drivers: STM32 81% market concentration in tracked FCs

co_occurrence_signal.pattern: STM32 concentration creates single-vendor risk across 81% of Blue UAS flight controller designs

event: Chinese ESC pricing increases 12-18% by Q4 2026 driven by compound demand from Drone Dominance, Ukraine FPV, and Hezbollah escalation creating supply competition across 119 China-origin ESC designs (72% of 165 tracked ESCs)
evidence_basis: Parts database shows 119/165 ESCs are China-origin (72%). Existing flag documents 'Ukraine and US Drone Dominance compete for same FPV components' including ESCs. New intel (April 11) reports 'Hezbollah is increasing FPV drone attacks' which validates 'ESC and motor supply chain stress'. DRAM pricing flag shows +8.5% QoQ precedent for component price increases under demand pressure.
probability_basis: Strong inference from documented demand competition (3 verified programs), China-origin concentration (72% verified), and DRAM pricing precedent (+8.5% QoQ verified). Price increase magnitude is estimated.
drivers: Hezbollah FPV escalation adding demand pressure

co_occurrence_signal.pattern: China-origin ESC dominance (72%) creates pricing vulnerability under compound military demand

event: High-energy laser C-UAS deployment at US-Mexico border drives beam director and targeting sensor procurement estimated $180-240M across Q2-Q3 2026, creating new demand category for military-grade optical components not tracked in current database
evidence_basis: April 13 Defense News article documents 'FAA and U.S. Defense Department have reached landmark safety agreement regarding use of laser weapons to counter unauthorized drones at US-Mexico border following safety assessment'. Existing flag captures regulatory precedent but misses procurement signal. Database shows 0 beam directors or laser targeting sensors tracked, indicating blind spot.
probability_basis: High confidence in deployment (FAA-DoD agreement confirmed), medium confidence in dollar estimate (inferred from typical C-UAS system costs without specific contract data), low confidence in component categories (discovery of untracked market segment)
drivers: Border drone threat requiring immediate C-UAS capability
Regulatory clearance enabling domestic high-energy laser deployment

lead_time_signal.component: High-energy laser beam directors and targeting sensors

event: Navy underwater drone mine clearance operations in Hormuz Strait validate autonomous maritime UAS battery demand spike — lithium-ion maritime-rated battery procurement estimated 8,000-12,000 units Q2-Q3 2026 for extended-endurance subsurface operations
evidence_basis: April 11 Defense Scoop article confirms 'Navy plans to use underwater drones in the coming days as part of its new effort to clear Iranian mines from the Strait of Hormuz'. Existing prediction flags 'validates autonomous maritime UAS procurement acceleration' but does not capture component-level impact. Database shows 219 battery SKUs with median $30 price but no subsurface-rated category, indicating supply chain gap.
probability_basis: High confidence in operation (CENTCOM announcement verified), medium confidence in unit estimate (inferred from typical mine clearance mission scope without specific platform count), battery type requirement is strong inference from subsurface operations
drivers: Iranian mine threat in Strait of Hormuz
Extended-endurance subsurface drone operations requiring specialized batteries

lead_time_signal.component: Lithium-ion maritime-rated batteries for subsurface UAS

co_occurrence_signal.pattern: Maritime autonomous UAS creates specialized battery procurement category not reflected in current 219-SKU database tracking

event: ELRS 900MHz receiver lead times extend to 12-16 weeks by Q3 2026 as Semtech LoRa chip allocation prioritizes automotive/IoT customers over drone market — impacts 329/391 ELRS receivers in database serving Drone Dominance and USMC FPV programs
evidence_basis: Existing flag documents 'Semtech LoRa SX1276/SX1280/SX1281 — 6 platforms, 5 programs, SINGLE SOURCE' with '329/391 ELRS receivers in Forge DB'. Flag notes Drone Dominance (200K drones) and USMC FPV ($47M) use ELRS. Database shows 132 control_link_tx with median $101 price. No lead time tracking exists for this critical single-source component despite documented program dependence.
probability_basis: Strong inference from single-source dependency (verified), documented program demand (verified), and typical semiconductor allocation patterns (industry knowledge). Lead time estimate based on similar chip shortage patterns, not direct supplier data.
drivers: Automotive/IoT market competing for Semtech allocation

lead_time_signal.component: Semtech LoRa SX1276/SX1280/SX1281 chips for ELRS receivers

co_occurrence_signal.pattern: ELRS dominance (329/391 receivers) creates Semtech single-point-of-failure across DIU Drone Dominance and USMC programs

event: Blue UAS compliance premium increases to 780-820% by Q4 2026 (from current 720%) as DRAM pricing (+8.5% QoQ), China ESC alternatives, and Semtech allocation constraints compound — reference ISR quad BOM rises from $4,348 to $4,680-4,750
evidence_basis: Existing flag documents 'Blue UAS BOM cost index: $4,348 — 720% premium over non-compliant build ($530)'. DRAM pricing flag shows +8.5% QoQ increase 'cascading to SBC costs' with specific Orin NX pricing ($599→$649). Multiple flags document STM32, ELRS, and ESC supply pressure. This validates and extends the cost index flag with compound effects not previously quantified.
probability_basis: High confidence in directional trend (multiple verified cost pressures), medium confidence in specific percentage increase (inferred from documented DRAM +8.5% QoQ and other component pressures), validated by existing $4,348 baseline
drivers: STM32 and ELRS lead time extensions increasing costs
Mesh radio costs ($1,800) stable but absorption pressure

co_occurrence_signal.pattern: Blue UAS compliance premium increasing from 720% to 780-820% as component supply constraints compound
